business.headline: How Sebi uncovered a Rs 15 lakh crore revenue hole at Rajesh ExportsA shareholder complaint, a Swiss refinery and years of unexplained revenues have culminated in one of the most significant accounting investigations in India's corporate history

Sebi's's Rs 15 lakh crore question: What went wrong at Rajesh Exports?Sebi's interim order has questioned whether Rajesh Exports backed its reported revenues with verifiable records. The findings have intensified scrutiny of disclosures, promoter-linked transactions and investor-facing accounts.

Gold is rising again: 3 things investors should knowGold prices are moving higher again after a period of weakness and attracting fresh buying interest. Here's what's driving the rally and what investors should keep in mind.

Rajesh Exports shares hit lower circuit as Sebi alleges 97-99% revenue inflationSebi's interim order against Rajesh Exports sent the stock to a 5% lower circuit after it alleged possible revenue inflation and barred promoter Rajesh Mehta. The action has heightened investor uncertainty as the regulator has sought more records and ordered a fresh forensic audit.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran-US tensions keep Dalal Street on edgeIndian benchmark indices opened lower as West Asia tensions and foreign selling weighed on sentiment. Elevated crude prices, FPI outflows and the RBI policy outlook remain key market triggers.

Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

Why file your ITR before July 31? These 3 benefits could helpWith the July 31 ITR deadline approaching, taxpayers have another reason to avoid delays. Filing your return on time can unlock several benefits that could prove useful throughout the year.

India responds to proposed US tariffs as trade pact negotiations continueWhile discussions over the tariff proposal continue, India and the United States are also working to finalise a broader trade agreement.

CCPA fines PhysicsWallah and McAfee for using dark patterns on digital platformsCCPA has also directed both companies to remove dark pattern practices that influenced consumer choices on their digital platforms.

Only 1% left to resolve in India- US trade pact, says US envoy Sergio GorSpeaking at Citi's 2026 India Conference on Wednesday, Gor said both countries were working to resolve the remaining "one per cent" sticking point and were hopeful of completing the agreement within the next several weeks.

Sensex ends 304 points lower as crude nears $100, IT stocks crackThe S&P BSE Sensex ended 303.67 points lower, or 0.41%, at 74,346.17, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 77.95 points, or 0.33%, to close at 23,405.60.

Big relief for airlines as govt caps jet fuel prices, clears Rs 10,000-crore fundThe decision comes amid recent developments in West Asia that have pushed up international ATF prices and increased pressure on airlines.

RBI MPC meeting starts today: Will rising inflation risks lead to a rate hike?The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) begins its three-day meeting today amid rising concerns over inflation, higher crude oil prices and global uncertainties. Will these factors be enough to trigger a rate hike?

Not correct: RBI rejects $12-billion gold sale report, says reserves unchangedIn a clarification issued on Tuesday, the RBI shared reports suggesting it had sold part of its gold holdings were "not correct" and highlighted that its physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes.

Want to use EPFO's UPI feature? Fix these 5 things firstImagine accessing your PF savings as easily as making a UPI payment. That's the experience EPFO is aiming to offer soon. However, the new feature will work smoothly only if your account details are accurate. Experts recommend checking five key areas now to avoid problems later.

Gold, silver prices are down again. Should investors buy, hold or wait?If you've been tracking gold and silver prices recently, you've probably noticed that the rally has lost some steam. Both metals have slipped despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, leaving investors with an important decision to make: buy more, stay invested, or wait for the dust to settle.

TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech crash up to 8%: Why are IT stocks falling todayIT stocks fell sharply on Wednesday as investors booked profits after a two-day rally. Weak ADR cues, cooling global software momentum and uncertainty over AI gains weighed on sentiment.

US proposes up to 12.5% tariff on India, China over forced labour concernsThe proposed action, announced by the Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), comes under Section 301 of the US Trade Act of 1974, the same law that was used to impose tariffs on Chinese imports during President Donald Trump's first term.

Sensex crashes 700 points. What's behind today's market meltdown?Markets opened sharply lower as renewed Iran-US tensions, higher crude prices and heavy IT profit-booking hit trade on Wednesday. The weaker rupee, persistent FPI outflow concerns and the RBI policy outlook kept volatility elevated.

Sensex, Nifty tank in early trade over crude oil price rise; TCS falls 6%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 763.45 points, or 1.02%, to 73,886.39 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 213.75 points, or 0.91%, to 23,269.80.

entertainment.headline: Singer Chinmayi on Delhi fire: Case will be dealt properly as victims are foreigners Singer Chinmayi Sripada reacted to the deadly Malviya Nagar fire in Delhi after 21 people died at a bed-and-breakfast property. She said that the case may be handled properly because several victims were foreigners.

Prime Video, ZEE5, Sony LIV this week: Gullak 5, Brown, The Pyramid Scheme and more This week's OTT lineup brings a diverse mix of crime thrillers, family dramas and romances, led by Brown, Gullak Season 5 and The Pyramid Scheme. From Karisma Kapoor's gritty cop drama to the return of the beloved Mishra family, there's something for every viewer.

Why Ucha Lamba Kad 2.O cannot match Katrina Kaif's original magic Ucha Lamba Kad 2.O may bring fresh beats and a new pairing with Akshay Kumar and Disha Patani, but it also serves as a reminder of one undeniable truth: some songs are impossible to separate from the stars who made them iconic.

Aamir Khan confirms marriage to Gauri Spratt: Ready to take it to next level Aamir Khan has confirmed that he and Gauri Spratt are set to marry on July 5. The news comes months after the actor had said that marriage was not on the cards for them.

Curry Barker expands world of Obsession with new film Anything But Ghosts Curry Barker has said Anything But Ghosts is set in the same universe as Obsession. The new film carries a direct reference to Nikki's fate, linking both stories.

Calm your wild thoughts down: Nushrratt Bharuccha breaks silence on viral clip Nushrratt Bharuccha has clarified that the background audio in her viral video was of a puppy crying at a friend's house. The actor also slammed the rumours and unfounded theories that surfaced online following the clip.

Ranveer Singh is truly a Dhurandhar: Film body member Upasana Singh amid Don 3 row Upasana Singh came out in support of Ranveer Singh as the Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) withdrew its non-cooperation directive against him. Also a member of Cine & TV Artistes' Association (CINTAA), she called the actor a true Dhurandhar.

Maa Behen review: A deliciously chaotic dramedy that gets its women just rightRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026 In Maa Behen, three women are thrown into chaos after a neighbour dies in their house. The dramedy uses gossip, crime and family friction to show how society misreads women.

Jennifer Aniston looks back at ex Brad Pitt's unforgettable Friends stint Jennifer Aniston and Lisa Kudrow recalled that many Hollywood stars, including Brad Pitt, were often nervous about appearing on Friends. Reflecting on the famous guest appearances, they said even established movie stars sometimes found it difficult to adjust to the show's unique rhythm.

Filmmaker and ex-CBFC chairperson Pahlaj Nihalani dies at 76 Pahlaj Nihalani, renowned filmmaker and former Central Board of Film Certification chairperson, has died at the age of 76. He was known for his contributions to the Hindi film industry and his controversial tenure at the CBFC.

Suchitra Krishnamoorthi reacts to Aamir Khan's third marriage, calls it 'brave' Suchitra Krishnamoorthi has wished Aamir Khan well amid reports of his marriage to Gauri Spratt on July 5. Her response also brought in her own reflections on divorce, remarriage and family dynamics.

Watch: Janhvi Kapoor marks Peddi release with a visit to Tirupati temple Janhvi Kapoor visited the Tirupati temple on the day Peddi released in theatres. Directed by Buchi Babu Sana, the film is a sports drama and is headlined by Ram Charan.

Netflix this week: Dhurandhar 2, Maa Behen, Office Romance and more Netflix's latest slate features a diverse mix of action, comedy, romance and thriller titles, including Maa Behen, Dhurandhar: The Revenge. From Madhuri Dixit's dark comedy to Ranveer Singh's intense action drama, here's what to add to your watchlist this week.

Sadhguru's taste test with The Vampire Diaries' Ian Somerhalder leaves fans amused Ian Somerhalder sat down with Sadhguru for a conversation on life, soil and regenerative agriculture, followed by a blind taste test. Their unexpected crossover, capped by light banter, drew amused reactions across social media.

Didn't do this for money: Shilpa Shinde on backlash over fake harassment charges Shilpa Shinde addressed the backlash over her podcast remarks on the 2016 Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain dispute. She defended her confession and clarified that she didn't do anything for money.

Peddi X reviews: Fans crown Ram Charan 'king', praise his brilliant performance Ram Charan's Peddi has sparked a wave of reactions on X after its theatrical release on June 5, with fans praising the actor's performance and calling it one of the finest of his career.

NGO seeks action against Shilpa Shinde over 'false' harassment claim Actor Shilpa Shinde has sparked controversy after admitting that her sexual harassment complaint against Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain's producer was false. Men's rights organisation to seek police action and triggered reactions from several members of the entertainment industry.

I attended a film press conference where the press never got to speak If journalists can't ask questions, is it really still called a press conference? Across film promotions, carefully curated interactions are replacing genuine conversations, with stars and PR teams controlling the narrative at every step. The result: plenty of publicity, but very little press.

Upasana joins fans, showers confetti at Ram Charan's Peddi screening. Video Upasana Kamineni Konidela was swept up in the Peddi fever as she watched the film with fans at a Hyderabad theatre. Videos show her cheering for Ram Charan and joining the confetti-filled celebrations inside the auditorium.

general news.headline: Heroes of Delhi hotel fire narrate stories of how they saved livesAt least 21 people died, and several others were injured after a massive fire tore through a B&B stay in South Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday. A short-circuit at the building is believed to have caused the fire as per initial probe.

TMC might find an unlikely saviour in Bengal. The BJP. Here's whyThe TMC's internal turmoil has delighted many in the BJP. But others in the party worry that if the Mamata Banerjee-founded party collapses entirely, the BJP might end up facing the rapidly reviving CPI(M)-led Left Front. Then there could be the Left-Congress combine too.

'Islam-friendly' gym in Kerala faces massive backlash online, owner clarifiesA gym in Kerala's Palakkad drew criticism after promoting itself as Islam-friendly with separate timings, spaces and trainers for men and women, and no music.

Monsoon storms into India via Kerala, IMD warns of heavy rain aheadThe official announcement of the monsoon's arrival comes after widespread rainfall and favourable atmospheric conditions satisfied the IMD's criteria, marking the beginning of India's much-awaited rainy season.

Peddi review: Ram Charan and the emotions hit sixes, the writing gets run outRelease Date:Thu Jun 04, 2026Peddi movie review: Ram Charan's Peddi follows a nameless village's fight for a railway station and identity. The film lands emotionally despite visible logic gaps, weak romance writing and uneven editing.

Newsrooms can moderate and calibrate, algorithms chase engagement: Kalli Purie on AIAt SPIEF 2026, India Today Group Vice-Chairperson and Editor-in-Chief Kalli Purie underlined that AI must support, not replace, newsroom judgement. Other speakers from Russia and China echoed concerns over conscience, cybersecurity and the risks of unchecked automation.

Groceries in 10 minutes, emergency services in 60: Delhi fire exposes cruel ironyDelhi fire: The rescue operation in the initial minutes at the B&B in Delhi's Malviya Nagar relied heavily on locals who risked their own safety to help those trapped inside. The incident has firmly put scrutiny on the alleged delay by emergency services.

He is 16. He broke into NEET and JEE portals to fix itAt just 16, Dubai-based CBSE student Rylen Anil has exposed vulnerabilities in both the NEET and JEE Advanced systems within days of each other. In an interview with India Today, he spoke about reporting the flaws, receiving a response from top officials, and why he believes young ethical hackers are helping make India's education systems more secure.

I finally got my passport, but I can't stop thinking about India's reputation abroadA first-time traveller says getting a passport has been overshadowed by worry over how Indian tourists are viewed abroad. The anxiety reflects a wider debate over viral incidents, perceived behaviour and the reputational cost for Indian travellers.

Foreign investors get major capital gains tax relief from the governmentThe Centre is weighing tax changes to make Indian government bonds more attractive to foreign investors. The proposed steps are aimed at boosting inflows, supporting the rupee and easing market pressure.

Laid mattresses, they jumped: How father-son duo saved 8 lives in Delhi hotel fireRiyazuddin Mansuri and his son Armaan Mansuri, who run a mattress shop in Hauz Rani area, helped people escape the Delhi hotel fire by laying mattresses on the road.

Aamir Khan confirms marriage to Gauri Spratt: Ready to take it to next levelAamir Khan has confirmed that he and Gauri Spratt are set to marry on July 5. The news comes months after the actor had said that marriage was not on the cards for them.

Maruti Suzuki WagonR flex-fuel version introduced, becomes India's first E100 carMaruti Suzuki has introduced the WagonR flex-fuel version, India's first flex-fuel passenger car, marking a major step towards cleaner mobility and increased adoption of ethanol-blended fuels in the country.

Tarique Rahman's first stop isn't India or China. Why he chose MalaysiaBangladesh PM Tarique Rahman will reportedly visit Malaysia on June 21. It would be his first foreign trip. Why didn't he choose India or China?

Calm your wild thoughts down: Nushrratt Bharuccha breaks silence on viral clipNushrratt Bharuccha has clarified that the background audio in her viral video was of a puppy crying at a friend's house. The actor also slammed the rumours and unfounded theories that surfaced online following the clip.

Kerala debt Rs 5.07 lakh crore: White Paper sparks ruling UDF vs LDF showdownKerala Chief Minister VD Satheesan tabled a White Paper in the Assembly outlining the state's financial stress. The document sparked opposition objections before the Speaker allowed it to be placed on record.

Kerala man travels 6 hours in auto-rickshaw to return US woman's lost ATM cardAn American woman shared how a local man in Kerala drove nearly six hours in an auto-rickshaw to return her lost ATM card, turning a stressful mistake into a memorable act of kindness.

Filmmaker and ex-CBFC chairperson Pahlaj Nihalani dies at 76Pahlaj Nihalani, renowned filmmaker and former Central Board of Film Certification chairperson, has died at the age of 76. He was known for his contributions to the Hindi film industry and his controversial tenure at the CBFC.

Accept rebel TMC MLAs or risk a party split. Mamata in Catch-22 bindA rebel group of TMC MLAs asked the Speaker to recognise Ritabrata Banerjee as a parallel leader in the Assembly. The move has sharpened a leadership contest around Mamata Banerjee and widened signs of organisational strain.

I attended a film press conference where the press never got to speakIf journalists can't ask questions, is it really still called a press conference? Across film promotions, carefully curated interactions are replacing genuine conversations, with stars and PR teams controlling the narrative at every step. The result: plenty of publicity, but very little press.

One salary, two paydays: Could Anupam Mittal's payroll idea work in India?Entrepreneur Anupam Mittal has reignited debate on whether India should shift from monthly salaries to a bi-monthly payroll system. While more frequent pay could improve cash flow for employees, experts argue it may not enhance financial discipline and could create legal, administrative and payroll challenges. The idea remains appealing but complex to implement.

Tourists perform garba at landslide-hit zone. Internet blasts themA video of tourists dancing Garba near a landslide-hit road has sparked criticism online. The clip has widened a debate on travel etiquette, civic sense and social media behaviour.

Short circuit caused Delhi hotel fire, faulty wiring under lens in prelim probePreliminary investigations into the Malviya Nagar hotel fire suggest a short circuit in the building's internal wiring may have sparked the blaze. Police have ruled out an LPG cylinder explosion, while forensic teams continue to examine the site and safety compliance measures.

Titan at 40: How JRD Tata's vision made an Indian watch brand globalA small village, a vision of Make in India, and a belief that ordinary Indians deserved a watch they could wear with pride helped shape India's own watch brand. It was a watch that gave the common man a sense of identity. JRD Tata was the man behind the idea, while Xerxes Desai, the company's first CEO, turned that vision into reality.

sports.headline: Ayush Shetty falls to Lee Chiuk Yiu as India crash out of Indonesia Open Ayush Shetty lost to Lee Chiuk Yiu in three games, ending India's last live run at the Indonesia Open. The defeat completed a poor week for the Indian big names, with Sindhu beaten, others out early and Sat-Chi withdrawing injured.

No Ashwin or Jadeja but Kuldeep backs India's new spin unit for Mullanpur Test IND vs AFG: For the first time in 15 years, India will play a home Test without Ashwin or Jadeja. Senior spinner Kuldeep Yadav backs a fresh, young spin unit to step up against Afghanistan in Mullanpur.

Legacy on the Line: 6 stars who must shine at FIFA World Cup 2026 FIFA World Cup 2026: These superstars cannot afford to slip up during the FIFA World Cup 2026 as their teams will depend on them heavily during the course of the competition. Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland will be expected to bring in the goals, while Lamine Yamal and Bruno Fernandes will provide the creative spark for their respective teams.

Group E preview: Can Germany reclaim former glory after successive early exits? FIFA World Cup, Group E preview: Germany may headline Group E, but Ecuador and Ivory Coast have the quality and belief to upset the established order. With World Cup debutants Curacao eager to make history, this promises to be one of the tournament's most competitive and unpredictable groups.

Vinesh Phogat one-ups WFI again as Supreme Court rejects federation's petition The Supreme Court dismissed WFI's petition against Vinesh Phogat's participation in the selection trials, calling it infructuous.

French Open Day 11 Schedule: Kostyuk, Andreeva meet amid Ukraine-Russia backdrop French Open 2026 Day 11 Order of Play: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk and Russia's Mirra Andreeva headline a fascinating French Open semi-final line-up, with all four remaining players chasing a maiden Grand Slam title in Paris.

Manchester United owners Glazer family considering sale of stakes at club Members of the Glazer family are discussing whether to sell some or all of their Manchester United shares, according to a report from Bloomberg. The talks could reshape control at Old Trafford after Jim Ratcliffe's investment and years of supporter anger.

Virat Kohli to miss ODI series against Afghanistan due to injury Virat Kohli will miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to a hamstring injury. Kohli suffered the setback during the IPL 2026 final where he helped RCB win their second consecutive title.

Indonesia Open: Familiar foe An Se Young proves too strong for PV Sindhu yet again PV Sindhu's Indonesia Open campaign ended in the second round as world No. 1 An Se Young claimed a 21-17, 21-15 victory, extending her perfect head-to-head record against the Indian to 10-0.

Are Gambhir and selectors on the same page over India's new T20I captain choice? Gautam Gambhir and the selectors are aligned on moving past Suryakumar Yadav, but not on who should lead India's T20I side next. Gambhir seems to have doubts about Shreyas Iyer as the new captain and has his own preferred choice for the role.

Indonesia Open Highlights: PV Sindhu loses again to An Se Young in 2nd round PV Sindhu vs An Se Young Highlights, Indonesia Open: An Se Young extended her dominance over PV Sindhu with a 21-17, 21-15 victory. The Korean claimed her 10th straight win over the Indian, sealing the match in just two games.

What's wrong with Smriti Mandhana? India need her firing for T20 World CupWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Smriti Mandhana has been well below her best in T20Is this year, and after another disappointing series against England, India will be eager for their star batter to rediscover her form and rhythm at the earliest.

ENG vs NZ Tests: Full schedule, squads, livestreaming and all you need to knowEngland kick off their home summer with a three-Test series against New Zealand, beginning at Lord's on June 4. Part of the ICC World Test Championship 2025-27 cycle, the series offers both sides a valuable chance to boost their standing, with England looking to rebound from a disappointing Ashes campaign and New Zealand aiming to build on their strong recent form.

French Open: Devastated Matteo Berrettini opens up after quarterfinal heartbreakMatteo Berrettini retired hurt during his Roland Garros quarterfinal against Matteo Arnaldi after suffering hip pain. The fresh setback ended his comeback run in Paris and left him speaking of his frustration with repeated retirements.

Pakistan Women face reality check before World Cup with Ireland series shockerPakistan Women endured a disappointing Ireland tri-series campaign, failing to reach the final after a washout against West Indies on Wednesday. They will now shift focus to preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 with two warm-up matches in Derby.

Arnav Paparkar's dream run ends with third round defeat in French Open boys singlesFrench Open 2026: Arnav Paparkar's promising Roland Garros 2026 Boys Singles run ended in the third round, as he went down 7-5, 6-2 to fourth seed Jack Kennedy in the Round of 16.

Flavio Cobolli fights past Felix Auger-Aliassime to reach maiden Grand Slam semisFrench Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli delivered the defining win of his career, rallying past Felix Auger-Aliassime 4-6, 6-4, 6-4, 6-4 at Roland Garros to reach his maiden Grand Slam semifinal.

FIFA World Cup: Switzerland's Embolo awaits US clearance after visa submissionSwitzerland forward Breel Embolo applied for an emergency U.S. visa at the American embassy in Bern on Wednesday, a day after he was denied boarding the national team's World Cup-bound flight because of a criminal conviction that recently became legally binding.

Praggnanandhaa relishes having a shot at title after ending Carlsen hopesFresh from his second win over Magnus Carlsen, R Praggnanandhaa said he was happy to remain in the title race as Norway Chess enters its final two rounds after the rest day, with the Indian Grandmaster relishing his chance to fight for the crown.

Want to quit tennis right now: Sabalenka drops a bomb after stunning exitRoland Garros: Aryna Sabalenka said she wanted to quit tennis after blowing a set-and-break lead to Diana Shnaider in the French Open quarter-final. Her remarks laid bare the mental strain of repeated setbacks on clay and grass.

Sooryavanshi is a special talent, should not change anything: Virat Kohli's coachSuperstar Virat Kohli's childhood coach, Rajkumar Sharma, has described teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as the "rarest of the rare" talent in T20 cricket, urging the youngster to stay true to his fearless approach and avoid making any changes to his game.

MPL helping state talent turn IPL dreams into reality: MPCA PresidentMadhya Pradesh Cricket Association (MPCA) President Mahanaaryaman Scindia said the addition of new franchises in MPL has opened up more doors for young cricketers across the state, giving them a larger platform to showcase their skills and accelerate their cricketing journey.

Mandhana inspires me, Richa Ghosh always supports me: Bengal's Ankita ChakrabortyAnkita Chakraborty said Smriti Mandhana inspires her while Richa Ghosh remains a trusted source of support ahead of the Bengal T20 League. The Adamas Howrah Warriors captain also highlighted how family values, Bengal's cricket structure and team balance shape her plans for the season.

World No.1 Aryna Sabalenka stunned as Diana Shnaider pulls off huge upset French Open Live Updates Diana Shnaider scripted one of the biggest upsets of Roland Garros 2026, rallying from 3-6, 3-5 down to stun World No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka 3-6, 7-5, 6-0 and reach her maiden Grand Slam semi-final.

Suryakumar to be sacked, Shreyas Iyer in 3-way race for India's T20I captaincyIs this the end of SKY era? Shreyas Iyer is the front-runner to become India's new T20I skipper for the Ireland and England series. The move would indicate a long-term leadership reset, with Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma also in contention.

Indian national anthem in Greece: Atiqa Mir races to most dominant win of her careerAtiqa Mir swept qualifying, heats and finals in Greece to seal her most dominant international karting win. The Indian anthem played on the podium as the 11-year-old underlined her world-stage promise.

Archer loves playing for England: Stokes defends pacer's IPL-enforced absenceEngland captain Ben Stokes has thrown his support behind Jofra Archer, saying the fast bowler remains fully committed to England despite missing the first Test against New Zealand due to his IPL commitments.

Mohammed Siraj bowls full tilt on hot day in Chandigarh, eases concerns over injuryIndia vs Afghanistan: Mohammed Siraj bowled at full tilt in oppressive New Chandigarh heat, easing fitness worries before India's Afghanistan Test. His spell strengthens India's plans with Jasprit Bumrah absent and selection calls still taking shape.

Group F preview: Samurai speed, total football, styles clash awaitsFIFA World Cup 2026, Group F Preview: The Oranje's Total Football meets Japan's high-speed press, Sweden's lethal attack and Tunisia looking to cause havoc. Group F is a tactical masterclass where the top dogs must deliver.

No Iga Swiatek, but Maja Chwalinska keeps Polish flag flying with dream runFrench Open 2026: With Iga Swiatek out of the tournament, qualifier Maja Chwalinska has emerged as Poland's unlikely new hope, reaching the Roland Garros semi-finals with a stunning win over Anna Kalinskaya.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
